name: How much compensation can I get for breathing problems from dust at my Boise factory job?

mainEntity.name: How much compensation can I get for breathing problems from dust at my Boise factory job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Compensation includes coverage for all medical bills and roughly 66% of lost wages. If the damage is permanent, you may receive a disability settlement based on your age, wage, and the severity of your lung impairment under Idaho's disability schedules.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Toxic exposure settlements vary wildly based on the severity of the illness and its impact on your ability to work. In Boise, compensation typically covers 100% of related medical expenses and roughly two-thirds of your lost wages while you are unable to work. If the lung damage is permanent, you may be eligible for a Permanent Partial Disability (PPD) award or even Permanent Total Disability (PTD) if you can never return to work in any capacity.

Valuation factors include:
- The cost of long-term treatments like inhalers, oxygen, or surgery.
- Your age and prior earning capacity.
- Whether you can be retrained for a different, "clean air" job.

Since Idaho laws have specific caps and schedules for disability, an "estimated" value is difficult without a medical rating. You should wait until you reach Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI) before discussing settlement. A lawyer ensures the insurance company uses a fair calculation that accounts for your lifetime medical needs.
